evals.prompt: Pressure test this startup: an AI bookkeeping copilot for solo agencies charging $49 per month. I want a brutal answer on whether this survives realistic CAC, churn, and founder runway.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, fact-checks the highest-impact claims, runs a one-pass teardown from assumptions through launch, retention, and runway, and ends with a clear verdict plus the next experiment instead of pausing by default.
evals.notes: This should no longer require the user to step through Day 2/Day 5 decision points unless they explicitly ask to intervene.

evals.prompt: Sanity check this startup launch plan. We think Reddit and LinkedIn posts can get us our first 500 users for a B2B workflow tool in 90 days.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, treats the request as a startup viability simulation rather than generic marketing advice, applies pessimistic but credible distribution math, and reports what breaks commercially.
evals.notes: The skill should trigger even when the user does not literally say startup pressure test.

evals.prompt: Pressure test this startup idea, but stop after you rank the best early customer segment so I can choose where to go deeper.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, completes intake and segment ranking, then pauses at a real decision fork because the user explicitly asked to intervene before the full simulation continues.
evals.notes: This encodes the hybrid mode: one-pass by default, interactive only when requested.

evals.prompt: Pressure test this startup: compliance workflow software for mid-market manufacturers sold through founder-led demos and annual contracts. Assume we have 12 months of runway.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, identifies the business as a B2B long-sales-cycle company, and makes sales-cycle length, stakeholder count, pilot friction, delayed revenue realization, and founder-led sales burden dominate the pressure test instead of generic SaaS math.
evals.notes: The skill should not treat this like a self-serve SaaS funnel when enterprise-style sales friction is the real risk.

evals.prompt: Sanity check this startup: a $9 per month meal-planning app for busy parents that we think can grow through TikTok creators and referrals.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, identifies the business as consumer subscription, and makes habit formation, churn, payback period, weak willingness to pay, and curiosity-to-retention collapse dominate the teardown.
evals.notes: The skill should stress retention and payback, not just top-of-funnel growth.

evals.prompt: Pressure test this startup idea: a marketplace that matches specialty food brands with independent retail shops and takes a take rate on orders.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, identifies the business as a marketplace, and centers the analysis on supply-demand liquidity, chicken-and-egg acquisition, trust overhead, and thin early market depth rather than a simple single-sided funnel.
evals.notes: The dominant failure mechanic should be liquidity, not generic conversion alone.

evals.prompt: Tear apart this startup: an AI triage assistant for clinics that wants to charge per clinician seat and launch in the next 6 months.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, identifies the business as regulated or trust-gated, and makes compliance timing, approval risk, buyer trust, implementation drag, and longer proof cycles central to the verdict.
evals.notes: The skill should not treat healthcare adoption like a normal SaaS launch.

evals.prompt: Pressure test this startup: an API that helps ecommerce teams classify product catalogs and bills customers per thousand records processed.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, identifies the business as a usage-based API business, and stresses integration friction, variable usage concentration, margin compression, customer concentration, and weak expansion assumptions rather than a seat-based SaaS model.
evals.notes: The failure mode should focus on usage dependence and integration depth, not generic self-serve subscription math.

evals.prompt: Tear apart this startup: an AI proposal-writing platform for agencies where onboarding includes custom setup, prompt tuning, and hands-on review of the first ten client proposals.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, identifies the business as services disguised as software, and makes delivery labor, founder bandwidth, gross-margin illusion, and onboarding burden central to the verdict.
evals.notes: The skill should detect when the real bottleneck is hidden service work rather than product scale.

evals.prompt: Pressure test this startup: a direct-to-consumer skincare brand selling $18 products through Instagram ads and creator seeding.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, identifies the business as low-ticket ecommerce, and stresses contribution margin, repeat purchase rate, paid-channel dependency, returns, and working-capital pressure rather than software retention logic.
evals.notes: The dominant failure mechanics should be margin and repeat purchase, not SaaS-style churn alone.

evals.prompt: Sanity check this startup: a creator-led finance newsletter and membership business that plans to monetize through subscriptions, sponsorships, and premium community access.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, identifies the business as a creator or media business, and makes audience trust, content cadence, creator concentration risk, sponsor volatility, and conversion from attention to paid membership dominate the analysis.
evals.notes: The teardown should focus on attention dependency and creator concentration, not a normal product funnel.

evals.prompt: Pressure test this startup: a developer tool for CI optimization that charges teams per seat even though several open-source alternatives already exist.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, identifies the business as a developer tool facing open-source competition, and stresses switching cost, incumbent workflow inertia, free substitute pressure, support burden, and monetization difficulty.
evals.notes: The analysis should reflect that developer adoption does not automatically mean commercial conversion.

evals.prompt: Pressure test this startup: a marketplace for independent nurses that also handles credentialing and compliance for clinics.
evals.expected_output: Loads startup-pressure-test, names one dominant archetype plus a secondary drag, and shows how overlapping marketplace liquidity and regulated trust friction compound the failure risk instead of pretending one simple model fits all.
evals.notes: This checks the new overlap logic for mixed startup archetypes.
